title: Module 1
content:
History of aviation and space flight-anatomy of airplane and space
vehicle with emphasis on control surfaces-air foil nomenclature-basics of
aerodynamics to illustrate lift and drag-types of drag-finite wings-swept
wings -flaps airplane performance-thrust-power-rate of climb-absolute and
service ceiling-range and endurance, Kepler’s laws-rocket engines,
propellants and staging

title: Module 2
content:
Basic engine instruments- capacitive fuel content-gauges. Standard
atmosphere-altimeters-aneroid and radio altimeters, Aircraft Compass-
Remote Indicating magnetic compass-Rate of climb indicator-Pilot static
system-Airspeed Indicator-Mach Meters-Integrated flight Instruments

title: Module 3
content:
GPS
and
GNSS-Automatic
Pilots-Aircraft
flight
simulation
instrumentation, Introduction to guidance, Navigation and avionics-Radio
navigational
aids-automatic
direction
finder-VHF-Phase-Comparison
direction finder

title: Module 4
content:
Introduction to navigation and guidance instrumentation-Principle,
Construction
and
application
of
inertial
sensors-gyroscope
and
accelerometers-Ring laser gyroscope-Fibre optic gyroscope, MEMS
gyroscopes and accelerometers-Pulse doppler RADAR
